methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
MightyFly is a Series A-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Manal Habib. The target sector contains 8 identifiable private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Rainmaker among them) and no public comparables, limiting direct market-based triangulation.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the company's $10M total raised as a valuation proxy. This method is applicable given the Series A stage and the absence of sufficient narrow-set peer data. The 2.0x multiplier reflects typical progression from seed/early to Series A closing valuations within frontier hardware sectors. This approach yields an implied valuation of $20M.

The peer-median method is not applicable. While four same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, none meet the threshold of three or more datapoints when filtered by stage proximity, valuation tier, or comparable maturity. Widening the filter to all-sector peers would risk introducing stage and business-model misalignment. Given this constraint, the analysis supports a triangulated range of $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median point of $20M. Key uncertainties include the absence of near-peer valuation benchmarks and limited visibility into MightyFly's revenue, customer traction, or technology differentiation relative to the broader cohort.
